01 · The Hide

It starts with one hand-selected hide.

Every Gioro piece begins life as a single full-grain hide, chosen for grain, character, and the way it will age — not for uniformity.

02 · The Cut

Cut by hand, not by machine.

Our artisans skive and shape each panel by hand, reading the leather's grain to decide exactly where every fold will sit.

03 · The Stitch

Saddle-stitched, edge to edge.

Two needles, one thread, pulled through by hand. It's slower than a machine — and it won't unravel the way a machine stitch can.

04 · The Finish

Finished with solid brass, made to outlast the leather.

Every snap and rivet is solid brass — set once, by hand, to hold for decades of everyday carry.

The Edit · Five Cuts

One silhouette, finished five different ways.

From leopard calf hair to deep crocodile emboss — every texture in the same compact H-fold frame.

Why Gioro

Built by hand, in small batches — not on a line.

Every Gioro piece starts as a single hide, hand-selected for grain and character. Our artisans skive, fold, and saddle-stitch each edge, then set solid brass hardware that's built to outlast the leather it's mounted on.

No two wallets are identical — the grain of the hide decides that. What stays consistent is the fold, the snap, and the feel in your hand.
